risk_if_not_implemented: Without Encryption for Outgoing Email, sensitive data may be exposed to interception or unauthorized disclosure, resulting in confidentiality breaches.

compensating_control_1.name: Email Content Protections
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to implement an email filtering security service to detect malicious attachments in emails and prevent users from accessing them.
compensating_control_1.justification: Email Content Protections (NET-20) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Encryption for Outgoing Email (NET-20.6) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Technology applicability. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Transmission Confidentiality
compensating_control_2.description: Cryptographic mechanisms exist to protect the confidentiality of data being transmitted.
compensating_control_2.justification: Transmission Confidentiality (CRY-03) provides cryptographic protection that compensates for the absence of Encryption for Outgoing Email (NET-20.6) by ensuring data confidentiality and integrity through alternative technical means. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
